metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_291
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The authors used purified synaptic vesicles isolated from rat cerebral cortex and investigated the effect of treatment with tetanus and botulinum neurotoxins on them. They observed that both tetanus and botulinum neurotoxin B are able to cleave Vamp2 (Fig. 1). Also, they reported that neurotransmitter release is inhibited by injection of tetanus toxin to Aplysia (Fig.4). This inhibition can be delayed by injection of a peptide that contains the cleavage site of tetanus toxin.. Experimental description: To provide direct evidence that tetanus (TeTx) and botulinum (BoNT) toxins are zinc proteases and to identify their intracellular target(s) we used highly purified small synaptic vesicles from rat cerebral cortex.

5/2/2018 Pim
Fig.4 is in Aplysia neurons, but using addition of a rat Vesicle-associated membrane protein 2 (Vamp2) peptide (see Fig.3).
